title: Platanthera bifolia lusus reichenbachiana
taxonomy: Platanthera bifolia lusus reichenbachiana is classified as a rare variant of the orchid species Platanthera bifolia. It is a subspecies within the genus Platanthera, which is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This variant is considered a regional form of the species, found in specific areas. The classification of P. bifolia lusus reichenbachiana is based on its distinct morphological and geographical characteristics. It is not a fully recognized species, but a variation within an existing species.

intro: Platanthera bifolia lusus reichenbachiana, also known as the Reichenbach's orchid or the lesser butterfly orchid, is a rare subspecies of orchid found in alpine regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Platanthera bifolia lusus reichenbachiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional remedies for respiratory issues and skin conditions.
